vectors.name: minimal_read
vectors.reason: Happy path: canonical form and SHA-256 are reproducible.

vectors.name: tool_call_with_counterparty
vectors.description: Action with counterparty TLS fingerprint bound into the record.
vectors.reason: Happy path: canonical form and SHA-256 are reproducible.

vectors.name: traced_child_action
vectors.description: Child action with trace_id + parent_id linking to a workflow.
vectors.reason: Happy path: canonical form and SHA-256 are reproducible.

vectors.name: tampered_signature
vectors.description: Signed bytes are unchanged but the ML-DSA signature was flipped by one bit. Verification MUST fail.
vectors.reason: ML-DSA-65 verify returns false when signature bytes do not match.

vectors.name: swapped_public_key
vectors.description: Signed bytes and signature are unchanged but public key was substituted. Verification MUST fail.
vectors.substitution: agent_public_key was replaced with an attacker-controlled key
vectors.reason: Verifier compares signature against the embedded public key; substitution breaks verification.

vectors.name: stale_card
vectors.description: Valid signature but card_signed_at is older than the verifier's freshness window.
vectors.reason: Freshness check: verifier rejects cards older than its configured window (recommend 5 min for interactive, 24h for cached).

vectors.name: nonce_mismatch
vectors.description: Peer sent nonce N1 with request; card echoes different nonce N2. Suggests replay or endpoint substitution.
vectors.reason: Nonce echoed inside the signed card does not match what the peer sent. Card was not produced for this peer's request.

vectors.name: card_version_downgrade
vectors.description: Card claims card_version a2a.v0 but the signature was produced over a2a.v1 canonical bytes. Tampered after signing.
vectors.reason: Because card_version is inside the signed payload, any attempt to rewrite it post-signing changes the canonical bytes and breaks the signature.

vectors.name: capture_topology_in_process_sdk
vectors.description: Audit Pack manifest entry stamped with capture_topology=in_process_sdk. Producer-side metadata; lives in `manifest`, never in the signed payload.
vectors.reason: Closed-vocabulary value; manifest-only placement preserves signed-payload bytes.

vectors.name: capture_topology_network_proxy
vectors.description: Audit Pack manifest entry stamped with capture_topology=network_proxy. Producer-side metadata; lives in `manifest`, never in the signed payload.
vectors.reason: Closed-vocabulary value; manifest-only placement preserves signed-payload bytes.

vectors.name: capture_topology_browser_extension
vectors.description: Audit Pack manifest entry stamped with capture_topology=browser_extension. Producer-side metadata; lives in `manifest`, never in the signed payload.
vectors.reason: Closed-vocabulary value; manifest-only placement preserves signed-payload bytes.

vectors.name: capture_topology_ebpf_observer
vectors.description: Audit Pack manifest entry stamped with capture_topology=ebpf_observer. Producer-side metadata; lives in `manifest`, never in the signed payload.
vectors.reason: Closed-vocabulary value; manifest-only placement preserves signed-payload bytes.

vectors.name: capture_topology_mcp_proxy
vectors.description: Audit Pack manifest entry stamped with capture_topology=mcp_proxy. Producer-side metadata; lives in `manifest`, never in the signed payload.
vectors.reason: Closed-vocabulary value; manifest-only placement preserves signed-payload bytes.

vectors.name: capture_topology_unknown_value_rejected
vectors.description: An unknown capture_topology value MUST be rejected by a conformant SDK before the HTTP roundtrip. Canonical bytes are still defined so implementers can verify rejection without depending on hash drift.
vectors.reason: capture_topology is a closed Literal of {in_process_sdk, network_proxy, browser_extension, ebpf_observer, mcp_proxy}; any other value is rejected client-side.
